| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf0954afe-c9de-4dad-8a63-7c4a9b9f7071/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: New State Laws, DEI Ban Challenged, and 16-Year-Old Turns Pro
body:
Gov. Greg Abbott signed 1,155 bills after this year's regular legislative session, and while 255 went into effect immediately, around 800 will take effect tomorrow.

| | |
---
| | |
# Sunday, August 31, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1839, the Rev. Thomas Spraggins organized [Independence Baptist Church](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAUAL8Gb7YAY5sMDF28pWtH62EeAZWABa9x8vdV8wvdbrqT7uu6XSGmmG-fcEdDT1-2jGdLSIT1jQSECc7mMFrIe4CUMG-Sy6nnuh_F5iCm16Y6Zyx3F9GhEcs0z6PmWnXBWLgoLkBxKbzni9LOqWmyTnotrB91F82fjOtRCMGF5MPslUK0M4zaLaMGpji6SKUOFJDwCc2nV3lgHfZy75IJREdj4weTK5PreZ4DtQAlp5hxKPXG5BogO1vKN5MrZqoRG5NrzA4PzA7zjr2Fg2Kjfg8_dQ9dNe44Y0qxzQtT_HaHmN45-t-kh5G_AQAA___tfn3G) in Washington County. Sam Houston was baptized there in 1854. Hurricane Katrina made landfall 20 years ago. Many of those who fled New Orleans found refuge in Texas, and thousands never returned. Today, we highlight the stories of some who have [adopted Texas](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDvO2zAQhE9DdTT40IMuWKRR96dNKazElUVkRQrkwrRvH9hXSDfAAB9mvj8_yw9EiunxG070_NrzkrBVQmYs38jASNBqwLhC2g4gwrQYZQapnLR6CRDpvfRd8KsKd92h19MwjtYp13eH3wY7rG4N9wGd1WEHBwOum1IQ3DQ420X_gSlntdZKG3Ub975HXEewLkzWgegVhsi53PhAxhfUnd75ieW25bMjfzBfVdhfwszCzK21W1q3sLdPLcz8OSHMTHkDEmbeSz5lwiZzIYRUJWeZcuFDftHyL3CJCaqM5wUby3rAFdNDUnxilUbJN0KpkoCxCDPbu9aTGoWZu-LD1SJRrOMkevU4IdJ3Iv-f2Kc3_wIAAP__6SqOrg) as their new home. We've compiled a list of [our favorite products and services that are having Labor Day sales](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAYBOCngS0WPxCMB4ZKlSu1SquoldLNwuYnRv1jRzYNydtXySt0u-G7G-6w63Y-UZqO7_6ELl_j3E1YVsKccXnE7DOSL2vA1PtpGD0RTp0UcrsRdqOgCz7RrdM8uF6EBjg6qLfGKCus5qOLtRLYyMZq3QSFWjcqxh6CVbE2egCe3H1MWAUAAqSoTNQasTde2VAr65kWGFKelyqPmPHq10i3-YJLNcwnTm7M-bwy9cRky2RbSqnKMvykqSe6CyZb-QIWYC8Nk-3h7fVz38Adq3b97YGp56_v9oMvLpxLIkqrqZkWx5NPjz7P__vl4uRfAAAA__9Z43QQ). Be sure to check them out in today's edition.

## New Laws Take Effect Tomorrow
Gov. Greg Abbott signed 1,155 bills after this year's regular legislative session, and while 255 went into effect immediately, hundreds more will [take effect tomorrow](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGOqzAMRb8m7IKckEK6yOJt2PVtZ4lMYkpUFypilfbvR3TmD2Z3pSsf-_jrMlwwc16u__FOQV7TOiy0FyYR2j5RUIhxL4nyiEuckZmWwYI9afC6MUPCzO_BVSmMkM6momC6U9s2Hryr5mCSjYQjeBdHawgstMl1FKcODJ59W-VwwMA3xhgwFup2co5obLHxqWs8KgeUsqxbLTMJvbBM_F6ftNVxvVccZpFHUc0_ZXtl-33f61tBOUpl-0NB2Z7XiKxsf6xStgd_ZP_T6w9TH5JaZhQteCNN00RR9LroQg_RRv-OVltIjz0z59J2ysH1jpk_l8jf_vcM9jsAAP__FQyCWw). Among the most high-profile is a new state-funded school choice program that allocates $1 billion for education savings accounts of around $10,000 per qualifying student. The state is currently [reviewing organizations](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2OhCAUxfGnwW4MIKNMQbGN3Wy7pbnIRUmuYuDufLz9ZnyF7f7Fya84P_fpDonSvnzDho5fMU87PishM5YzGRgJnjVg8rDPKxDhPmmprxdpL52aAiR6T6YJzstwUw06NVz7vrPSmmZ1Nl69N0b5GYYeZ7wBDjHq4eY1Ko2xSe6DSdsppaTSsu2jMYi-h86GobMgjMSQOJeWV2R8QY30zg8s7Zy3htzKfFTRfQk9Cj3OeTu4ZCIs7Tlul_wQejxKXgpsVegRw-8MnPL-6QpCj01x4XgmolT7QRi5bJDo5Pl_pzyc_gsAAP__6Sl4GA) to implement the program. Other education bills, including optional prayer time and annual crisis plan reviews, require district votes, [while lawsuits challenge](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2q4zAQRp9G7sbo70q6hYo0bkIWli22NBNrHAtkOdizcfL2i_0KtzswcJjz_b31N8wl18cvnCnye1z6SvtWiJnWExmZCu5bonzHOkxYCtVeS_0FMoBRfcJcPr1tUrzL9K0aisp_OWeCDLaZokObaPz2PvghKa99CNbY4AY3OpOCb3I8ZDIYpZRUWrZutJbo7tCE5E1AYSWlzMva8kRMb9zG8lletLbDMjclTszPTZiL0J3Q3b7v7bzV4yZ0RxX-bUJ3R4nQ3ckTrQS4EvBEUGmH0wlnKRypwBMyPBbIlRegcaSBYV5qwo_QHa5wuahr_nP93awxPfdcSt6cF1Y-Zszl_Ip_tuUr6v8BAAD__3pfiTA) laws mandating Ten Commandments displays and banning gender identity instruction in schools. Lawmakers also approved [laws affecting homeowners](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2uqzAQhVdjOpD_MKZw8Rq6vPaWaMBDsDTYCEYh2f0V2cLtRqOjT-c7P4_xAYlSfv6HDQO_lzJmvE5CZjy-JwMjwXVGTBPkeQUizKOWuq2lr40aIyT6jLaKYZKxVxUG1bXOGS-9rdbQtt5rcLZ1EF2nvO-7KCNO1vrWGr1UKdww6Y1SSiotG7dYizg5MD52xoOwEmPicjS8IuMbzoU-5YVHM5etorAy76cw_4QehB6u62q2zwkZMpecyh0SerhVhB6ozEBCD3uhxGm-X3BwmgmFHr7sOuNV38L1iTvXqtZKqt71ptnXvTpC3K9ElE7XCSufGyT61uC_jfcK-jcAAP__cDiDbg), including regulating lot sizes in cities, prohibiting Homeowners Association citations for dead grass during watering restrictions, and mandating a database on local bond elections. The Department of Public Safety is getting a new Homeland Security Division, the [Texas Cyber Command](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAMgJ8mbKDESZMwZPiXbP3XG5EhpkRyoYKoad_-RO_e4Dbrs_RZn7-uwxUz5_X2H-8UymvehpXqwVQK7Z-xYCHGeiTKI67Tgsy0DiDh0krfajUkzPweTJPCKFOvGgrKXazVXnrTLEFaO3lDowNnZ5SWKCXdYz-jVhqUa3I4ZdJrpZRUIDs7G0M0WtQ-Oe1RGEkpl23vykKFXnjM_N6etHfTdm84LKU8DqH_CYgCYq21W8cpzfVcC4hnhIDI24QsIH4E7S9cqbY_4ExsD3qUVgmIulfK9RcBsdlDetTMnA_rhJG3O2b-HC5_e9czwHcAAAD__3qRfgE) is being established at UTSA, and the Texas Grid Security Commission will evaluate threats to the power grid. Citizens of hostile nations will be banned from owning land in Texas.
## Katrina Survivors Share Stories 20 Years Later
Hurricane Katrina [struck near New Orleans](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2upDAMRp8mdEH5YZhQpNiG7m67JTKJGaINCUp8LzNvvwqvsJ1l-3w-8p-v5QtCDOn1Gw609N7ykvCqEYmw3CUBYYSregwrJLdDjJgWJdSDC8O1XDyE-FmGzttV-El2aOXzMY7aCDN0u1XCTDAJo82kR202J5w2zusB5fgQXnTBtjBhtJRSSCX6cRsGxHUEbfxTG2CDQB8ol552JHxD3eIn_2DpXT66aHeiszL9i6mZqfm6rt6ttam3OVPzjTA1txZTc8EtoqOQXjwnTjvycJzgiO_fpQQHCflfoBIS8IgbtaUjxBhyqjwkF799Q2s-kLu18jud43HG_EFsB7pi_Xk1pI5PNojXASHervR_H_6x6l8AAAD__-TPk54) on Aug. 29, 2005, killing nearly 1,400 people and causing $125 billion in damage. Many refugees who fled to Texas still call it home 20 years later. Kym Adams fled with her two boys, ages 15 and 17, packing only enough for a few days away from home. Unlike the 25,000 or so evacuees who [found shelter](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O4yAQRZ8Gd1j8xSYFxTbusu2W1hjGMQo2EcyG5O1XjvYNvu5oNDoz9_65zTeIKR7337Cjo_ea5wNbTUiE5YsEhAlaDRgXOPwGKeExK6EuXFiu5Rwgps9suuAWEa6yQyfHyzBoK6zpNmcWf9FWW-m1EEEKs4yrH8cwDNaOa9BddKdMWC2lFFKJfliNQVwG0DaM2gIzAkOkXHrakPANdU2f_MLS-7x3yW1Ez8r0L6YmpqbWWu9T9A-15b-V8nFuMTWdWZiaUvaQmJrOm0xNwp58_Q74B6FUDith4Q-gEg_gW278v6hyqFRyyDvyBT3syIEvCD4fPK98y09kauqKC88WU4p1GJkR9x1i-n5KP-v35dS_AAAA__-W5Y8f) in the Astrodome, Adams stayed in a motel. "_I still have tremendous survivor's guilt that I worked through for that_ ," [she explained](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkb2O4yAUhZ8GdzfizzYpKLZxN9tuaV3gekBDYAQknrz9KnmF6Y50pCN95_v3sX9gyql8_sUb2fFz1L3Q2TONQe0dBw7KePZAyWHxEXOmsksuZ-AGlNgDpvzc9RSs4-EqJrJinZdFGW70FG1YF3WYlZOi2aEhQYeTq1ZIgrhAmpJ9jXGjhBBcSH5ZDq2J3ILKhFUZZJpTSKO2y4g06Af7kZ_1Qe3i623KNo7x3Zn6w-TG5Hae5-Ur1vurZHLDNpLPxOT2gmFyy9VjZnKL99aSx0LwhaOlgtDv7ZEetcGRSugQCV_HAJYAhU6I9UaQCsR676MWkByehK1DxkGNyU2aGdR19m7mV-DKEegQHFy5QxBrCIv0QhgvpmbD95lyTn1ZmeafN0z5zTJ-Z-Bh5f8AAAD__zZtmxg). Quanise Thomas was an emergency services dispatcher in New Orleans when the storm hit, and she eventually settled in Plano. Her father was rescued from a rooftop, and her mother was missing for a month. For many people, [she admitted](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGO3CAURb8Gd4zwA2OmcJHG3aZNaT3geY2CYQQv652_j2aUP0h3pSudq6vz62P7wJRT-fyJJy38vdet0NUzMVN7R0amjFePlDyWcGDOVDZQMEnlpB63iCk_NzPExat4HwdaxnmyVjvlzHAsRs_6rtHr4IlctAHv8zTZQLOJu4N9SMsLppwex1GNoG52N4bIW9QuztqhMIpi4tpufBDTN_Y9P-sXtVuo55CXg_nRhf4hYBWwXtd1u3bEVylgxcYpZBKw9meJKSCnWgSslVnA-hu5pYISlIC11MaHfA0UiSXKvbaTmix0ydoyYekypv5ADgc12Shgzl0ef1pLAQvJfzABK7hZajehjwaltR6kuZso0VuQXgPqXWmr4zi0JT6ulHPqdhZGfZ6Y8vsV_5-LrwX-BgAA__9LMZ6o), "_my voice was the last voice that they heard_." Rodney Boisley worked at a New Orleans hospital that lost power. The patients were moved to the rooftop, where those who survived [waited five days](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL-O4yAQxp8GdxMBdmxSuLjG3V57pTWBSUA7gRXM2uu3PzmvsN1P-qTv37-P9QMTp_z8iy-a5edR1kx7YxKh-kZBIca9BUp3zD4iM-XVansF7aA3a8DExzp0Yb7rcDMdzWa6jmPvtBu6OIcHkrH66q_jdO-NnlD3NzN6P5FDmlyX5tNMu94Yo43Vl_ExDET3EXsXpt6hGjSFJKVeJJLQD7YHH2WjevHl1fEcRb6a6v8ouyi77Pt--Wwop6jsck5QduHikZVdzihlF-1Ovim7xO9ak8dM8IlSU0agDf03UYNKHpmBCbeUnxDLiwBzgD0eIJEOaIIHBUgZGmbALCWnAlbDQVgbMApVZZeuzuFrT8ypjZMa9POFid_d5XePb7P9HwAA__94rpZH) to be rescued. He moved to San Antonio, where his brother served in the Air Force.
## No. 1 Texas Falls, Lone Star State Opens '25 Season
Top-ranked Texas [lost 14-7](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2KhDAURp8mdiP5M4lFim0CW8y2W8rV3GjgakTDOPP2y8wrbPfBB-fA-b0Pd8iUt_kHVvT1mcqw4XUS1orHZ1aoSHCdEfMI27QAEW6D5LK7cXdTYoiQ6TXoJvqRx1406IXtjFGOO90s3ll0nepT7DhwY5VJWkbArrdJIHdjk_0bxp0SQnAheWuS1oijAeWiVQ6Y5hhzLUdbF6z4hDPRqzzwaKeyNuSXWveTqS8mA5Phuq4Wz317n0yGqRDhjLdUSh2BiMlw4AQ7k2FgMsyw4ndkMmgubCeNtc3h435lonwayzSfV8j0MdX_9Xl4-RcAAP__70Z4Vw) at No. 3 Ohio State to open the 2025 season in a rematch of last year's Cotton Bowl semifinal game. The Longhorns were [stopped twice on fourth down](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUBeCnwVsiuGC4HhjaSmxJxnazLuE6RiJ2ZNP8vH3lvEK3ozN80jnfh_5AueTpcqQr-_oc5n7ix1q4Vl7esVLlQo81cY40nUcqhaceJLQ7iTut-kS5vHrTJB9l6lTDXrnWWo0STTN6NAlkbM80SBc7sK2KzrkIsVWJOsQm-w2TqJVSUoHc28EY5mhJY3IaSRjJKdd52deRKz9pHcprvvOyP8_Xpvix1tsq9IeAICA8t1ZA-Aqfp2M4_QgI24bfVUBQnVVoW7TQaQ0I6Eyz-HR75FLyap0w8nKlXN5w_d8ddw9_AQAA__8DLXBE) inside the Ohio State 10 during the second half. Quarterback Arch Manning finished 17-of-30 passing for 170 yards, with one touchdown and one interception in his first road start. No. 19 Texas A&M hosted in-state UTSA, with quarterback Marcel Reed returning for his third year in College Station. The Roadrunners enter 2025 on a run of five consecutive winning seasons. clean_body: Gov. Greg Abbott signed 1,155 bills after this year's regular legislative session, and while 255 went into effect immediately, around 800 will take effect tomorrow. | | | | | | Sunday, August 31, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1839, the Rev. Thomas Spraggins organized in Washington County. Sam Houston was baptized there in 1854. Hurricane Katrina made landfall 20 years ago. Many of those who fled New Orleans found refuge in Texas, and thousands never returned. Today, we highlight the stories of some who have as their new home. We've compiled a list of . Be sure to check them out in today's edition. Abilene 🌤️ 82/70° | Amarillo ☁️ 85/64° | Austin ☁️ 88/77° | Brownsville ☁️ 98/79° | Corpus Christi ⛈️ 95/79° | DFW 🌦️ 88/74° | El Paso 🌤️ 92/70° | Houston ⛈️ 87/74° | Laredo ☁️ 100/78° | Lubbock 🌦️️ 85/68° | Midland ☁️ 90/70° | San Antonio 🌧️ 98/77° | Tyler 🌧️ 82/73° | Waco ☁️ 89/73° Take The Flyover with you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | New Laws Take Effect Tomorrow Gov. Greg Abbott signed 1,155 bills after this year's regular legislative session, and while 255 went into effect immediately, hundreds more will . Among the most high-profile is a new state-funded school choice program that allocates $1 billion for education savings accounts of around $10,000 per qualifying student. The state is currently to implement the program. Other education bills, including optional prayer time and annual crisis plan reviews, require district votes, laws mandating Ten Commandments displays and banning gender identity instruction in schools. Lawmakers also approved , including regulating lot sizes in cities, prohibiting Homeowners Association citations for dead grass during watering restrictions, and mandating a database on local bond elections. The Department of Public Safety is getting a new Homeland Security Division, the is being established at UTSA, and the Texas Grid Security Commission will evaluate threats to the power grid. Citizens of hostile nations will be banned from owning land in Texas. Katrina Survivors Share Stories 20 Years Later Hurricane Katrina on Aug. 29, 2005, killing nearly 1,400 people and causing $125 billion in damage. Many refugees who fled to Texas still call it home 20 years later. Kym Adams fled with her two boys, ages 15 and 17, packing only enough for a few days away from home. Unlike the 25,000 or so evacuees who in the Astrodome, Adams stayed in a motel. "I still have tremendous survivor's guilt that I worked through for that ," . Quanise Thomas was an emergency services dispatcher in New Orleans when the storm hit, and she eventually settled in Plano. Her father was rescued from a rooftop, and her mother was missing for a month. For many people, , "my voice was the last voice that they heard." Rodney Boisley worked at a New Orleans hospital that lost power. The patients were moved to the rooftop, where those who survived to be rescued. He moved to San Antonio, where his brother served in the Air Force. No. 1 Texas Falls, Lone Star State Opens '25 Season Top-ranked Texas at No. 3 Ohio State to open the 2025 season in a rematch of last year's Cotton Bowl semifinal game. The Longhorns were inside the Ohio State 10 during the second half. Quarterback Arch Manning finished 17-of-30 passing for 170 yards, with one touchdown and one interception in his first road start. No. 19 Texas A&M hosted in-state UTSA, with quarterback Marcel Reed returning for his third year in College Station. The Roadrunners enter 2025 on a run of five consecutive winning seasons. See the box score . No. 16 , with Kevin Jennings starting at quarterback after leading the Mustangs to last season's College Football Playoff. | Peter,
The American public is about to become intimately familiar with the term judicial retention but by the time you see this phrase on the news it will be too late.
Each decade Pennsylvanians vote to retain (reelect) their Supreme Court justices. This November, the three democratic justices that make up the Democratic majority on the PA court are up for retention and MAGA has launched a full court press to remove them.
This may seem like a parochial issue that will only matter to PA voters but we can assure you that the GOP, who is spending Presidential election type money to remove these judges, does not see it this way.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0017XiJV2xftK6c-vRvPorAedOhz-g23c2AO40WUDmavexfax2EgdUCnmAs7e2nMWNrNNblxgTpfTamFw_X_P2-iugx9zujLCVjhLkeAHFhBlGW91VydSwzn5MuRvs7lSNqYrLVXg6pEhqzij-_Wf0__LZsM7d7PcAYM4DyokFDk9N_qgx2MOY716KEYxo3ovmGyG6rsJeMXPu3sG96ImDASRyaGQhwEZmIDi7nhUUc3MUrLZagRO6ltQ==&c=lndoJWtiVxUTnF8s_yaC1EeyONvwj5Wsh46y638sq1JvRRTadCIZvw==&ch=R566_3rNuQRy11XWtvq2iKh6zuSklzJZo6i74SwDOrBBv-P-IcpYlg==)
---
| Losing means GOP control of key swing state PA for the next decade and beyond. That means:

* 19 electoral college votes guaranteed for President Trump when he seeks a third term in 2028.

* The erasure of all Democratic districts from PA's congressional map in time to save GOP midterm prospects for 2026.

* An extreme abortion ban imposed on Pennsylvania women.

This is the straw that breaks the camels back. A deathblow for American Democracy. Millions of lives ruined. Fascism triumphant.
And a loss here is exactly what Democrats are on track for. Saddled with astronomical campaign debt from the Kamala Harris campaign, the DNC has all but declared bankruptcy, and will be in no position to help us mount a comeback. All the while MAGA is up on TV, has thousands of canvassers, and is poised to pull off a shocking win.
[Defend Our Courts is ringing the alarm bells and is scrambling to mount a desperate defense. We can rally but with just 65 days left time is running out. clean_body: This is the straw that breaks the camels back. A deathblow for American Democracy. Millions of lives ruined. Fascism triumphant. | | | | | == | Peter, The American public is about to become intimately familiar with the term judicial retention but by the time you see this phrase on the news it will be too late. Each decade Pennsylvanians vote to retain (reelect) their Supreme Court justices. This November, the three democratic justices that make up the Democratic majority on the PA court are up for retention and MAGA has launched a full court press to remove them. This may seem like a parochial issue that will only matter to PA voters but we can assure you that the GOP, who is spending Presidential election type money to remove these judges, does not see it this way. | | == | Losing means GOP control of key swing state PA for the next decade and beyond. That means: 19 electoral college votes guaranteed for President Trump when he seeks a third term in 2028. The erasure of all Democratic districts from PA's congressional map in time to save GOP midterm prospects for 2026. * An extreme abortion ban imposed on Pennsylvania women. This is the straw that breaks the camels back. A deathblow for American Democracy. Millions of lives ruined. Fascism triumphant. And a loss here is exactly what Democrats are on track for. Saddled with astronomical campaign debt from the Kamala Harris campaign, the DNC has all but declared bankruptcy, and will be in no position to help us mount a comeback. All the while MAGA is up on TV, has thousands of canvassers, and is poised to pull off a shocking win. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == Everything is on the line here. | While Gov. Hochul Is Busy Rigging Elections, NY Taxpayers Bear The Burden
Rob Ortt
New York taxpayers continue to struggle with rising costs and a deteriorating quality of life in their communities.
[The Daily Wire](https://u4b9iicbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001LcIEenbso5zVBs0yXa58z3THet1nnxSi2wSTwqF8APqXsK3L6feRUI3A_qoMk0QZ6D2WKi-geDNI-Dco4XpDnlpj0mcp2O10kyUTEchXnh6ZMj6f9LQBrDe9ibcUkNxhCDqq04eCaq6Xu_yEAJraPJaXktiDLRNXanR2BfhWkSO98E2CvRNmwKL9-fayxcHLGwEvGcPtcy0eftu1qYIqpFBrffIAm7zrG29K3iIjeY1RtNV1TaCws8k3_HSgpv8F&c=lWfNhCLyLerkNUIkuBDyhfBtqBujyTfELoUIS7OMQNM9qELuBkClAg==&ch=vIT0YgIouy6N_d4BHFgd7_hl1sUR2gvZtxmMJ76iMguAnJuIxDJrfQ==)
Governor Kathy Hochul's administration recently blew nearly $500 million in potential savings for New York taxpayers by failing to move seniors to a federally funded Medicare plan. That's half a billion dollars -- gone -- because Kathy Hochul couldn't get her act together. That's money that could have gone to tax relief, infrastructure, and keeping your utility bills lower. When it comes to utility bills, National Grid customers in upstate New Yorker should brace themselves for a 15% increase in their bill starting next month. This amounts to about $216 more per customer per year, thanks to Governor Hochul's green energy scam agenda. Likewise, Con Edison has proposed double-digit rate increases that will raise costs for downstate residents, driving up their utility bills as early as January 2026.
And that's just the average cost increases. Many families and small businesses will be paying far more at a time when they are already struggling and can't afford any more increases in their cost of living and doing business.
These increases are not a reflection of market forces or a sudden spike in demand, rather, they are a direct result of action by the governor's Public Service Commission, which Hochul controls. This board approved the increases to fund her ambitious climate mandates. It's typical of Albany: self-righteous politicians pushing you to "do your part" by paying more, even when you're already struggling.
Why are these things happening? For starters, Kathy Hochul is incompetent and instead of addressing these problems and protecting you, she prioritizes politics. Currently, she's focused on her crusade to illegally gerrymander New York's congressional map - a scheme that the courts, and voters, already rejected. When the rules don't work for the Democratic Party, Hochul tries to change them.
Hochul and her cronies describe mid-decade redistricting as "undemocratic," yet in the same breath she and her fellow New York Democrats have declared they will engage in mid-decade redistricting. In other words - their words - they're going to take undemocratic actions in order to "save democracy" in America. The hypocrisy and absurdity of this is unparalleled, and New York voters can see right through it. The voters and the courts in this state have already made it clear the people must have the ability to choose their representatives, rather than politicians manufacturing uncompetitive districts and forcing their preferred candidates down everybody's throats.
New York voters overwhelmingly supported a nonpartisan redistricting process. However, Democrats ignored them, blatantly gerrymandered districts, and when they were thrown out by the courts, they corrupted the Court of Appeals to get their way. Now, they are looking to stomp on the Constitution again for another shot at rigging the system to protect their power. Meanwhile, New York taxpayers continue to struggle with rising costs and a deteriorating quality of life in their communities.
This is not leadership. It's political wheeling and dealing at its worst, and taxpayers are footing the bill.
Albany's ruling class has made their priorities clear: your paycheck, your bills, and your future come second to their grip on power.
Until we get a governor who will put people over politics, rein in spending, protect ratepayers, and actually focus on making New York affordable again, New Yorkers will keep paying for Kathy Hochul's failures.
______
Rob Ortt is the Republican Leader of the New York State Senate. He represents the 62nd Senate District.

name: Sign Your Name | Left Action
subject: Sign: Tell JD Vance: “Take your thoughts and prayers and shove them”
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Tell JD Vance: “Take your thoughts and prayers and shove them”](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcb77b5ed-97c6-4c30-9e56-2e9689296021/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1d6e39ac-ea99-444b-a4c1-b5d11287a3fe/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Another horrific shooting, and once again, Republicans don't do a damn thing except offer "thoughts and prayers." And now JD Vance [insists that HE is actually the victim](https://default.salsalabs.org/T58cacc96-7c3c-46e9-9c67-4c86fa3fe288/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), because people rightfully pointed out that thoughts and prayers are meaningless without action. And as someone who pretends to pray, this hurts his feelings. [**Add your name, and tell Vance to "Take your thoughts and prayers and shove them."**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T106ecda1-ca6d-4779-9551-b8367f011924/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Vance isn’t doing a damn thing to prevent gun violence. So no, he doesn’t get credit for closing his eyes and staying silent (which is also what he does when presented with real solutions that would save lives). **[So let’s send a message loud and clear: Vance doesn’t get to lecture us while he enables the carnage. Tell him: “Take your thoughts and prayers and shove them.”](https://default.salsalabs.org/T57013aec-a4fe-414d-afc8-2ce63741756c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**
---
| | | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta0a7ffa5-8d2d-4d9f-abb5-dc091c0ed105/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Thank you, Left Action

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: "Record scratch" moment in SEPTA saga
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

Stay up to date on all of this and more when you[ subscribe to our daily email list ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=b471abbd46&e=b7b3eddce2)and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[SEPTA is broken, not broke](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4f7411fd01&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Guy Ciarrocchi_
SEPTA is broken. “Team SEPTA” wants us to believe the issue is that they’re broke (and deserve even more taxpayer aid), but the sad reality is the system is broken.

Nothing that SEPTA officials, its Board of Directors comprised of politicians, its politician allies, mass-transit cultists, anti-car activists, climate warriors, or their apologists in the legacy media — and, even those honest to goodness riders genuinely concerned about getting to work — can say will change that unfortunate reality.

**Why It Matters**. Almost 84 percent of SEPTA’s budget comes from taxpayer subsidies, not riders. And they still can’t make it work — and have shown little willingness to embrace reality and change.

SEPTA is pretending to be “broke” to cause crises to get more taxpayer money. They have $400 million in reserves and millions more in designated taxpayer funding. The present system cannot be supported without a never-ending, ever-growing annual subsidy from non-riders needed to prop up an outdated, non-customer friendly, bureaucratic, politically operated, unaccountable system.

They want money, not reform. Democratic politicians — from Shapiro to the state House, to suburban county commissioners — are leading that cheer. The reality is that the Republican-led state Senate actually passed a compromise bill funding SEPTA through the summer of 2027, but Team SEPTA didn’t like how it was funded. So, here we are.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ae6f95660e&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[N](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9b0c39a1a1&e=b7b3eddce2)[o budget, no pay](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=2311a996c2&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Kyle Sammin_

It is rare nowadays to look to the city of Philadelphia for a lesson in good governance. But that wasn’t always the case. In the 1950s, a brief ray of good-government sunshine penetrated the jungle canopy of corruption that usually shrouds the City of Brotherly Love. The jungle soon reasserted itself, but we still have the memory of that light enshrined in the Philadelphia City Charter of 1951 — or, at least, in those parts of it that still remain from those days.

I’ve written before about how the state and federal governments should a#2 dopt Philly’s resign-to-run law. This summer, another section of the charter has seemed especially useful: if the city council’s redistricting — required every ten years following the decennial census — is not done on time, city councilmembers do not get paid.

Now, Pennsylvania’s legislature doesn’t have a problem getting redistricting done on time, at least not since the state supreme court usurped that power in 2018. But they do have an awfully hard time finalizing a state budget. Maybe we should take a page from the playbook of Joe Clark, Dick Dilworth, and Walter Phillips by amending the state constitution so that if there’s no budget, [there’s no paycheck](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=2425efe783&e=b7b3eddce2).

**Why It Matters**. Here in Pennsylvania, the budget deadlines are fake. Even the real ones, like the SEPTA funding, are [kind of fake](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=6a16074375&e=b7b3eddce2) — SEPTA has reserve funds, but they’d rather make commuters’ lives difficult to prove a point. With no pressure points, what would anyone compromise? We are nearly two months overdue on this budget and there is no sense of urgency, not from the House, the Senate, or the governor.

So we owe it to our state to make a pressure point. No work, no pay. Let’s amend Pennsylvania’s constitution to cut off legislators’ pay — and the governor’s, too, for that matter — when they don’t get the job done on time. Like the reformers who briefly succeeded in cleaning up Philadelphia’s government, we must demand real constitutional change to clean up the continual mess in Harrisburg.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0e36125ad9&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Frank Speidel: SEPTA, the American way, and free stuff](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=8740436f9a&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Garrity consolidates PA GOP leaders around gubernatorial campaign](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=26b1de7919&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Lawsuit ends with ‘Black Doctors Directory’ opening to all physicians](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=87070cda46&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Stew Bolno: Mozart, Picasso — and Trump?](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7dc446361f&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickles: Gregory Tony’s rise — and how a 1993 Philly homicide disappeared ](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e24d490d16&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Ben Mannes: Trump signs order to end cashless bail](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=bbf77e8368&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Guy Ciarrocchi: Moderate Democrats are not moderates](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3de37e1941&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. What we're reading**
It is sometimes said that “California is what America will be in 15 years.” This was once an optimistic statement to make, but it has increasingly become a portent of doom.

But is it true? No, probably not any more. There was a time when Califrnia was at the cutting edge of the American Dream, the new, beautiful, prosperous society where everything worked and every kid had a chance to make good. Now, though, the vaulted institutions are failing, middle-class people are priced out of homes, and one-party rule has turned the American Dream to a California nightmare. Many are moving on to greener pastures.

But as [Joel Kotkin writes in _City Journal_](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=680f592a57&e=b7b3eddce2), they are bringing with them the seeds of the destruction that drove their old home state into destruction. “Not long ago,” he says, “Colorado, Washington, and Oregon were widely hailed as states with bright futures.” Now, imported blue state policies promise the same sclerosis that took the lustre from the Golden State.
---
|

If you’re reading this, you already know we’re the only place you can find certain stories in Pennsylvania—the ones other outlets won’t touch, the ones that dig deeper, and the straightforward political news that keeps you ahead of the spin. Now, we’re bringing that news straight to you — faster, clearer, and without the noise.

clean_body: News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | | | | | Good morning and welcome to Broad + Liberty 's Weekly Reads. Stay up to date on all of this and more when you get the best of Broad + Liberty delivered straight to your inbox. | | 1. By Guy Ciarrocchi SEPTA is broken. “Team SEPTA” wants us to believe the issue is that they’re broke (and deserve even more taxpayer aid), but the sad reality is the system is broken. Nothing that SEPTA officials, its Board of Directors comprised of politicians, its politician allies, mass-transit cultists, anti-car activists, climate warriors, or their apologists in the legacy media — and, even those honest to goodness riders genuinely concerned about getting to work — can say will change that unfortunate reality. Why It Matters. Almost 84 percent of SEPTA’s budget comes from taxpayer subsidies, not riders. And they still can’t make it work — and have shown little willingness to embrace reality and change. SEPTA is pretending to be “broke” to cause crises to get more taxpayer money. They have $400 million in reserves and millions more in designated taxpayer funding. The present system cannot be supported without a never-ending, ever-growing annual subsidy from non-riders needed to prop up an outdated, non-customer friendly, bureaucratic, politically operated, unaccountable system. They want money, not reform. Democratic politicians — from Shapiro to the state House, to suburban county commissioners — are leading that cheer. The reality is that the Republican-led state Senate actually passed a compromise bill funding SEPTA through the summer of 2027, but Team SEPTA didn’t like how it was funded. So, here we are. | | 2. By Kyle Sammin It is rare nowadays to look to the city of Philadelphia for a lesson in good governance. But that wasn’t always the case. In the 1950s, a brief ray of good-government sunshine penetrated the jungle canopy of corruption that usually shrouds the City of Brotherly Love. The jungle soon reasserted itself, but we still have the memory of that light enshrined in the Philadelphia City Charter of 1951 — or, at least, in those parts of it that still remain from those days. I’ve written before about how the state and federal governments should a#2 dopt Philly’s resign-to-run law. This summer, another section of the charter has seemed especially useful: if the city council’s redistricting — required every ten years following the decennial census — is not done on time, city councilmembers do not get paid. Now, Pennsylvania’s legislature doesn’t have a problem getting redistricting done on time, at least not since the state supreme court usurped that power in 2018. But they do have an awfully hard time finalizing a state budget. Maybe we should take a page from the playbook of Joe Clark, Dick Dilworth, and Walter Phillips by amending the state constitution so that if there’s no budget, . Why It Matters. Here in Pennsylvania, the budget deadlines are fake. Even the real ones, like the SEPTA funding, are — SEPTA has reserve funds, but they’d rather make commuters’ lives difficult to prove a point. With no pressure points, what would anyone compromise? We are nearly two months overdue on this budget and there is no sense of urgency, not from the House, the Senate, or the governor. So we owe it to our state to make a pressure point. No work, no pay. Let’s amend Pennsylvania’s constitution to cut off legislators’ pay — and the governor’s, too, for that matter — when they don’t get the job done on time. Like the reformers who briefly succeeded in cleaning up Philadelphia’s government, we must demand real constitutional change to clean up the continual mess in Harrisburg. | | 3\. Lightning Round | | 4\. What we're reading It is sometimes said that “California is what America will be in 15 years.” This was once an optimistic statement to make, but it has increasingly become a portent of doom. But is it true? No, probably not any more. There was a time when Califrnia was at the cutting edge of the American Dream, the new, beautiful, prosperous society where everything worked and every kid had a chance to make good. Now, though, the vaulted institutions are failing, middle-class people are priced out of homes, and one-party rule has turned the American Dream to a California nightmare. Many are moving on to greener pastures. But as , they are bringing with them the seeds of the destruction that drove their old home state into destruction. “Not long ago,” he says, “Colorado, Washington, and Oregon were widely hailed as states with bright futures.” Now, imported blue state policies promise the same sclerosis that took the lustre from the Golden State. | 5\. Download our app! If you’re reading this, you already know we’re the only place you can find certain stories in Pennsylvania—the ones other outlets won’t touch, the ones that dig deeper, and the straightforward political news that keeps you ahead of the spin. Now, we’re bringing that news straight to you — faster, clearer, and without the noise. The Broad + Liberty mobile app is here, and they’re the fastest way to get Pennsylvania’s real news. ! | | | Broad + Liberty is funded by readers like you. Your generous support ensures that Broad + Liberty can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com. As always, gifts made to Broad + Liberty are 100% tax deductible! Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you. name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: reaching out to you
body:
|
#
Friend, I know it’s Labor Day weekend – and I hope you're getting a chance to slow down and spend quality time with loved ones. I also wanted to make sure you see the headlines this week made clear just how consequential our election is for control of the House. As _Fox News_ put it: **_“[Top of mind for Republicans is] Michigan’s 8th Congressional District, represented by freshman Rep. Kristen McDonald Rivet.”_** That’s Republicans going on the record that flipping MI-08 is key to their plans to win control of the House. And they’re putting their money where their mouth is – spending nearly a million dollars on attack ads already this year in an attempt to tear me down. I know Election Day is still more than a year away, but what we do right now matters. The truth is, early grassroots support right now sets the tone for the entire campaign. Every dollar we raise today helps us build the resources to reach voters, push back against GOP lies, and prove this campaign has what it takes to win. As of this morning, we’re still shy of where we need to be heading into September. A donation of $3 from you right now would get us back on track. We’re standing with the California Democrats as they fight back against Texas Republicans’ sham redistricting and put a fair map on the ballot.

We’re defending 21 of the 26 toughest frontline seats that will decide control of the House.

And we’re winning voters over with commonsense solutions — **lowering costs, defending Social Security, and protecting voting rights.**

_But to secure our victory, we need a strong grassroots movement behind us!_

name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: Make Alberta Great Again
body:
Considering secession can lead the province to unlocking its potential

# [Make Alberta Great Again](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=172186319&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyMTg2MzE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY2NDU2MzEsImV4cCI6MTc1OTIzNzYzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7kemj0w_u0CooBVXHr0GRjrQgTsR81JSnlxOcRe13GI)
### Considering secession can lead the province to unlocking its potential
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Aug 31
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=172186319&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyMTg2MzE5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjY0NTYzMSwiZXhwIjoxNzU5MjM3NjMxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.bZCEy6r_WlCunG0-t_l4pH6NK80zb4A8_iMzHlcLB6o&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=172186319&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyMTg2MzE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY2NDU2MzEsImV4cCI6MTc1OTIzNzYzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7kemj0w_u0CooBVXHr0GRjrQgTsR81JSnlxOcRe13GI&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=172186319&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyMTg2MzE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY2NDU2MzEsImV4cCI6MTc1OTIzNzYzMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7kemj0w_u0CooBVXHr0GRjrQgTsR81JSnlxOcRe13GI)
---
| | [](https://substack..ng8C7rOku68uQWw4s72-TzlfGRmusc_Se2FUITatUCE?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/make-alberta-great-again?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f67d8377-b849-44f3-8d8c-94dac0797a39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Last week, I was in Alberta, Canada to give a keynote speech at the Energy Business Forum organized by the [Canadian Energy Executive Association (CEEA)](https://substack.com/redirect/8d99cd9d-bd2a-4f91-a2f2-d97acfc7f1f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It gave me a chance to speak with fellow speakers Rick Perry, the former Governor of Texas and former U.S. Secretary of Energy, and Danielle Smith, the Premier of the Province of Alberta. I also had an opportunity to speak with business leaders, representatives of First Nations, as well as current and former government officials. And even though this wasn't my first trip to the province (I've been dozens of times and count the Calgary Stampede as one of my favorite events...anywhere in the world!), I was again struck by the gracious people, clean air, and stunning beauty.
And potential. I mean **LOTS** of potential.
Alberta is teeming with energy resources (hence the site of an energy forum) and it accounts for most of Canada's production and resources. In 2023, it was responsible for 84 percent of Canada's crude oil and 61 percent of its natural gas; the province's oil sands alone account for [97 percent](https://substack.com/redirect/22655444-0b82-4a49-8492-446c5a7dc8d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the country's proven oil reserves. There's an also significant coal and the province has massive solar and wind potential.
I haven't even begun to describe the significant non-energy resources in the province: there are [massive known reserves of potash, lithium, gold, diamonds, iron, limestone, salt, and timber](https://substack.com/redirect/ef13c96e-b776-4c07-9c1b-174af9e88cf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as well as dozens of other industrial minerals. [Alberta is also endowed with significant rare earth materials](https://substack.com/redirect/a371dc8e-9b3f-4aad-b4d6-7561607a84b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that are increasingly needed for defense and electrification applications.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7ad0607f-a5e7-47f5-9a8d-51915660e66c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Oddly enough, it's this abundance of resources that has been a source of tension between province and the federal government. Eastern political leaders, in the grips of climate hysteria, have sought to stifle the province's energy industry through environmental regulations, emissions caps, carbon taxes, and the cancelation of pipelines. The federal government even [banned](https://substack.com/redirect/e1f2ae33-6d65-43ec-ad16-3bb27c528746?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) tankers from loading oil off of the country's west coast, strangling exports to Asia.
Not surprisingly, these long-simmering tensions have led many to call for more provincial autonomy, and some have even suggested secession. As I mentioned above, one of my co-speakers at the event was Danielle Smith, Alberta's current premier and a vocal proponent of Albertan autonomy. Her campaign slogan was "Alberta First" and as premier, she has championed the [Alberta Sovereignty Act](https://substack.com/redirect/52be6563-96e7-407e-a1d9-5a0abb44ca45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which allows the province to [defy](https://substack.com/redirect/2323c3e1-3d0c-4556-99bb-cdcacd5115d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) federal laws it doesn't agree with, especially when it comes to energy.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..xfuEseZJIQ2RXHeqoZ9nPdG15naRhbiCjPbN5qiQel0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=172186319)
And although she is opposed to outright secession, she has also made it easier for that to occur. Smith's government passed a [bill](https://substack.com/redirect/ce32983a-54fc-402b-a6ac-512700fa6f56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that lowered the number of signatures required to trigger a provincial referendum, which immediately kicked off a [grassroots movement](https://substack.com/redirect/4665e4b1-4ab6-4e06-a368-e7612785a211?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to do exactly that. The secessionist movement has its roots in the 1980's, and although there has been an ebb and flow to its support over the years, only recently has it picked up serious momentum; a recent poll showed that [36%](https://substack.com/redirect/77d75fe3-67c4-4ea1-9709-756778b37f5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Albertans wanted to secede.
Joining the United States isn't necessarily part of the [plan](https://substack.com/redirect/511d0f94-1273-45a2-9095-e03d22b7fdef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (secessionist leaders instead [champion](https://substack.com/redirect/4665e4b1-4ab6-4e06-a368-e7612785a211?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) economic integration with America), but Alberta probably would have few other options. Partitions are typically acrimonious (my parents fled one-such partition when British India was cleaved in two) and bad blood with their former countrymen would leave the land-locked province little choice but to seek the military, political, and economic protection of the United States.
* * *
## [The Pursuit of Better](https://substack.com/redirect/c1e98c7f-525f-46cf-8e87-30b35d10f16f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| June 18, 2024
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c1e98c7f-525f-46cf-8e87-30b35d10f16f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[While celebrating Father's Day with my own children this past weekend, I couldn't help but think about my own father. He's an amazing individual who embodies the adventurous spirit and can-do attitud...](https://substack.com/redirect/c1e98c7f-525f-46cf-8e87-30b35d10f16f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/c1e98c7f-525f-46cf-8e87-30b35d10f16f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
And the economic benefits alone would be tantalizing. As part of the United States, free of Ottawa's onerous regulations, Alberta would boom as the province was allowed to develop its amazing endowment of natural resources. The combined country would be a global energy powerhouse, allowing the U.S. to finally pivot from the Middle East.
What about the rest of Canada? With Alberta out and part of the U.S., other provinces would probably reconsider the merits of staying part of Canada. Might they seek to join Alberta and gain a star on the American flag? Quebec might be inspired to give secession another try. And the Ottawa elites, without the much-needed economic contributions from the west, might be forced to reconsider their current policies. The old Albertan [taunt](https://substack.com/redirect/84832166-1296-4c4b-bed4-0c23ebce6fa0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) would finally come to pass, as they would have no choice but to "freeze in the dark."
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..xfuEseZJIQ2RXHeqoZ9nPdG15naRhbiCjPbN5qiQel0?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=172186319)
While the actual combination of Alberta and the United States is an admittedly small to remote possibility, it may nevertheless be worthy of pursuit. With Donald Trump in the White House, Premier Smith might find a receptive audience for the discussion and might even be able to negotiate a good deal for Albertans. And if she can present her constituents a compelling proposition, how would Ottawa react? Would other provinces follow Smith's lead and also seek statehood?
Regardless of whether Alberta secedes, or upon secession, joins the United States, the **mere act of exploring the possibility will improve Alberta 's position** in Canada and the world. The debate itself would bring Alberta's potential into focus, making its economic value obvious to everyone. Premier Smith would be seen as an open-minded leader who is not constrained by conventional thinking. And in the process, the province would be moving towards realizing its enormous potential.

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/3cff7580-f27a-49a5-8ba3-edf265a3be1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/5aacd104-fec6-4aa3-a0f9-accba2d9f789?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/4653c6ee-908e-4d19-82b7-e5f1a0bb3faa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

clean_body: Considering secession can lead the province to unlocking its potential | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Considering secession can lead the province to unlocking its potential | | | Aug 31 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Last week, I was in Alberta, Canada to give a keynote speech at the Energy Business Forum organized by the . It gave me a chance to speak with fellow speakers Rick Perry, the former Governor of Texas and former U.S. Secretary of Energy, and Danielle Smith, the Premier of the Province of Alberta. I also had an opportunity to speak with business leaders, representatives of First Nations, as well as current and former government officials. And even though this wasn't my first trip to the province (I've been dozens of times and count the Calgary Stampede as one of my favorite events...anywhere in the world!), I was again struck by the gracious people, clean air, and stunning beauty. And potential. I mean LOTS of potential. Alberta is teeming with energy resources (hence the site of an energy forum) and it accounts for most of Canada's production and resources. In 2023, it was responsible for 84 percent of Canada's crude oil and 61 percent of its natural gas; the province's oil sands alone account for of the country's proven oil reserves. There's an also significant coal and the province has massive solar and wind potential. I haven't even begun to describe the significant non-energy resources in the province: there are , as well as dozens of other industrial minerals. that are increasingly needed for defense and electrification applications. | | ---|---|--- Oddly enough, it's this abundance of resources that has been a source of tension between province and the federal government. Eastern political leaders, in the grips of climate hysteria, have sought to stifle the province's energy industry through environmental regulations, emissions caps, carbon taxes, and the cancelation of pipelines. The federal government even tankers from loading oil off of the country's west coast, strangling exports to Asia. Not surprisingly, these long-simmering tensions have led many to call for more provincial autonomy, and some have even suggested secession. As I mentioned above, one of my co-speakers at the event was Danielle Smith, Alberta's current premier and a vocal proponent of Albertan autonomy. Her campaign slogan was "Alberta First" and as premier, she has championed the , which allows the province to federal laws it doesn't agree with, especially when it comes to energy. And although she is opposed to outright secession, she has also made it easier for that to occur. Smith's government passed a that lowered the number of signatures required to trigger a provincial referendum, which immediately kicked off a to do exactly that. The secessionist movement has its roots in the 1980's, and although there has been an ebb and flow to its support over the years, only recently has it picked up serious momentum; a recent poll showed that of Albertans wanted to secede. Joining the United States isn't necessarily part of the (secessionist leaders instead economic integration with America), but Alberta probably would have few other options. Partitions are typically acrimonious (my parents fled one-such partition when British India was cleaved in two) and bad blood with their former countrymen would leave the land-locked province little choice but to seek the military, political, and economic protection of the United States. | | | | ---|---|--- | June 18, 2024 | And the economic benefits alone would be tantalizing. As part of the United States, free of Ottawa's onerous regulations, Alberta would boom as the province was allowed to develop its amazing endowment of natural resources. The combined country would be a global energy powerhouse, allowing the U.S. to finally pivot from the Middle East. What about the rest of Canada? With Alberta out and part of the U.S., other provinces would probably reconsider the merits of staying part of Canada. Might they seek to join Alberta and gain a star on the American flag? Quebec might be inspired to give secession another try. And the Ottawa elites, without the much-needed economic contributions from the west, might be forced to reconsider their current policies. The old Albertan would finally come to pass, as they would have no choice but to "freeze in the dark." While the actual combination of Alberta and the United States is an admittedly small to remote possibility, it may nevertheless be worthy of pursuit. With Donald Trump in the White House, Premier Smith might find a receptive audience for the discussion and might even be able to negotiate a good deal for Albertans. And if she can present her constituents a compelling proposition, how would Ottawa react? Would other provinces follow Smith's lead and also seek statehood? Regardless of whether Alberta secedes, or upon secession, joins the United States, the mere act of exploring the possibility will improve Alberta 's position in Canada and the world. The debate itself would bring Alberta's potential into focus, making its economic value obvious to everyone. Premier Smith would be seen as an open-minded leader who is not constrained by conventional thinking. And in the process, the province would be moving towards realizing its enormous potential. | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and **. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. You're currently a free subscriber to . body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj206xtc59ohmw0bdif4c36d86z9qwmfit3zwxkqyefirdguldozzzcv6xyyd2p46xnnzesjsk2b769cqnrjx&__stmp=t1v092&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1k63f5dc966mtoabyfzukxe4sbgcfqbn1bfuoz4jq30i1qtsu2blo6nwetpxnesqkdtxtpeke67rfmghj5vwuxu6t3jobmh3tsq398eitaw9i4pnouvya8l6ldqqstzp4q2n5zopbg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1k63f5dc966mtoabyfzukxe4sbgcfqbn1bfuoz4jq30i1qtsu2blo6nwetpxnesqkdtxtpeke67rfmghj5vwuxu6t3jobmh3tsq398eitaw9i4pnouvya8l6ldqqstzp4q2n5zopbg&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t1v092&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: need your opinion ASAP (via Fight for Reform)
subject: The Kennedy Center - named after MELANIA TRUMP!?!?!
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG) **_Breaking from The Hill:_** [**HOUSE GOP MOVES TO RENAME KENNEDY CENTER’S OPERA HOUSE AFTER MELANIA TRUMP**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=3&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG) [**DO YOU SUPPORT RENAMING THE KENNEDY CENTER OPERA HOUSE AFTER MELANIA TRUMP?** [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=5&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG)
| | | [YES ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=6&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG)
---
| [NO ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=7&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG)
---
10,000 RESPONSES NEEDED FROM YOUR STATE BEFORE MIDNIGHT MAGA Republicans are full of over-the-top ideas to glorify Donald Trump and his family:

⭕ Adding his face to Mt. Rushmore...

⭕ Making his birthday a national holiday...

⭕ Renaming the Washington, D.C. subway the “Trump Train”...

_But they’ve stooped to a new low with this one._

The Kennedy Center Opera House is a historic institution honoring President Kennedy. Slapping Melania’s name on it is nothing but an embarrassing attempt to curry favor with Donald Trump.

But friend, this isn’t just silly — it’s dangerous. **If we don’t push back, they’ll keep rewriting history in Trump’s image.** [**If we’re going to stop this, we need to prove that Americans from Arkansas to Arizona agree. So please, friend, let us know what you think below:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=8&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG) [**DO YOU SUPPORT RENAMING THE KENNEDY CENTER OPERA HOUSE AFTER MELANIA TRUMP?** | | [YES ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=10&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG)
---
| [NO ➞](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/67079?t=11&akid=23342%2E4449941%2EqEVgyG)
---
Thanks for your input,

Sadly, this is nothing new to me. I've borne _venomous_ insults and been called so many _horrible_ names throughout the years.
The "tolerant" Left will turn racist and ugly the instant a black woman decides to reject the Democrat party and embrace common sense. Liberals just can't wrap their heads around the fact that I dare to think differently, that I am not beholden to their political agenda simply because of the color of my skin.
I'm the sitting Lieutenant Governor, second in command in the former Capitol of the Confederate States. I'm an immigrant, a Marine, and above all, a human being. There is no place for this disgusting hatred in our nation. I won't let their insults define me, and I certainly won't let them slow me down!
I didn't become the first black, female Lt. Governor of Virginia by playing the victim. I did it by embracing my conservative values and trusting in God!
Leftists can spew whatever racist nonsense they like, but I will win the race for Governor of Virginia with you on my side.
Here's my call to action: [Can you chip in to help us spread our positive conservative message?](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=4548e6cc3c004d7dab63057792b612c0&_e=vNl7r912ttywfQY2tet9FgDcn_0Q9w6tiFPS--wn8oAIDpnBgg9OABM7uElAOC5UHa67kSazBvTn4kgDyNFyBn2y2djSo6QrlBSUiGTOcyET5OVbnpcnyTb5jU6xtIP0LyuxYmHQbxSckHVarMnQyiSeNvY63cuOEy5VVHYW8DurWT3RvtZJBeXnVQjES1FVr-HnQY8gyKwl-fWWWswdvTs4xfXoJ6U8SblmyszxE6mpTpJe28J0xpMkUvRcsnPzHz9w1PQJQK6PlbUp_CSrAHYYKVK8PfG1Ey-I0M5gIOqKBCsA9vXO-Tzlk1L4b-6ffKLqiPf_e6qGz4QiDcDdM2Qx9oXrgk76p3mpyjOC-WvVwcnP9k3dMnhRD_ORhz6ChOtSi85mmYp4vU_dVEdWerkAxFgnLdZnCWYm791Z-J0fnPPhMio8eCI7xausi304Tuv0TynvjdYDy6I78bhQfqCL9YX8TFwfKBfcsDp78sgOV2kctFulSQRPSNbNlZOZJPPByCKer1uHgLTTRpmQOhGyG1yxaxvjDV4QnoStGzRBI36fJ6fXtNPvG3oI5mRcCIXPM4ir31XkWuoR6vFYcnLioUn1Gdk2KSldIUFIrDSN8dshgL2kruDV-N1_KC9fMMxfsNvRtqTwjkNCwG9XHbsXM5KlUkSCpK4czBoxVa0%3D)[ DONATE $18 OR MORE NOW >>

clean_body: | | | | | is a sign that a racist protester held up as I spoke to the Arlington County School Board about keeping our girls safe.Fellow American - I'm disgusted, but not surprised. Sadly, this is nothing new to me. I've borne venomous insults and been called so many horrible names throughout the years. The "tolerant" Left will turn racist and ugly the instant a black woman decides to reject the Democrat party and embrace common sense. Liberals just can't wrap their heads around the fact that I dare to think differently, that I am not beholden to their political agenda simply because of the color of my skin. I'm the sitting Lieutenant Governor, second in command in the former Capitol of the Confederate States. I'm an immigrant, a Marine, and above all, a human being. There is no place for this disgusting hatred in our nation. I won't let their insults define me, and I certainly won't let them slow me down! I didn't become the first black, female Lt. Governor of Virginia by playing the victim. I did it by embracing my conservative values and trusting in God! Leftists can spew whatever racist nonsense they like, but I will win the race for Governor of Virginia with you on my side. Here's my call to action: back against the liberal media and the entire Democrat establishment is never easy, Fellow American. With patriots like you on my side, I know we will win in November and beyond. Thanks for your support. God bless you, Winsome Earle-Sears (R-VA) | | | I'm Winsome Earle-Sears, Republican candidate running for Governor of Virginia. I broke records as the first black female Lieutenant Governor in Virginia, but my mission isn't over yet! It's going to take all of us conservatives working together to win. | | Paid for by Winsome for Governor Military disclaimer: Neither military info nor photographs of Winsome Earle-Sears in uniform imply endorsement of Winsome Earle-Sears by the Department of Defense or its particular military departments. body:
[**Peter, I’m humbly asking you for $5 before my end-of-month fundraising deadline — split between Shontel Brown and me.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114346358/566725135/-330283573?refcode=EM_F_250831Split_N_ES_90DO_8_NG_N_L1&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzN2U0ZTg3Yi02Yzg2LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZpBfJeF90rwHzJ66qSkkBbXp4obCGOeO0eE_6aIU6Gc=)

🔵 I’m running for re-election in one of the most competitive districts in the country;
🔵 The top election experts are calling this race a TOSS-UP; and
🔵 Vice President JD Vance and the national GOP are already attacking me.

That’s why _your_ support is so important today.

Your $5 will go toward countering the misinformation Republicans are spreading about my record. It’ll set the record straight about my work to lower gas, grocery, and housing prices. And it will get us on track to keep OH-13 blue and flip the House.

$5 may not seem like much compared to the mega-millions Republican super PACs will spend on this race. But if everyone reading this pitches in, we’ll meet our $25,000 goal in no time.

name: Team Kelly
subject: What was on your mind when you woke up this morning?
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
We bet that first thought wasn’t about making a donation to Mark.
#

Good morning, friend.
What was on your mind when you woke up this morning?
We bet that first thought wasn’t about making a donation to Mark.
But today, right now, at this exact moment, it’s what we need you to do. Honestly, it’s really important. Could you give us a chance to explain why?
We all know what Trump’s doing. He thinks he’s a king, and he’s definitely acting like one.
The one sure way we can put limits on his power is by retaking Congress next year.
Mark’s a Senator, so he’s got a lot of work to do in Congress. But he’s also the Vice Chair of the DSCC, which means he’s got a huge role to play winning back the Senate.
What all that means is that a lot of people are looking to Mark for leadership, hope, and help.
Mark is set on doing anything he can that might check Trump’s authoritarian power grab. But the single most impactful thing he can do is support the candidates running for Senate so we can take back our majority.
That’s why this donation is so important. So we can help Mark get the job done.
So, if you care about retaking the Senate and defending the country from Trump, we’re humbly asking:
[**Will you make a donation to Mark Kelly? He’s coming up on an important deadline at midnight tomorrow, and he needs our help to keep up the fight.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-aug25/.JGniHS&refcode=em250831-1-700k&t=1&akid=6169%2E6688874%2E46IeGb)

I need to level with you. We already have the plan for what we need to do in this campaign to reelect Mark next year. We also know how much we need to raise to ensure that plan can be executed.

**But tonight is our deadline for August fundraising, and we’re still falling short of our $75,000 online goal. We can’t afford to miss it – because the truth is, if the GOP flip this seat, there’s no path to a Democratic Senate majority.**

[_**Will you donate $5 or more before midnight tonight to help reelect Mark and take back the Senate next year?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114347747/566729042/172610512?amount=5&refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_EOM6_250831_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZWQwNjJlNC02Yzg2LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=F4W0QpJNu2RJ9GwYn3sEWJmwYVcwkDioQYV2iX3-XRs=)

I know it might seem like coming up short one month isn’t a big deal, but that couldn’t be further from the truth. Next month is our FEC filing deadline, where our fundraising numbers will go public for all to see.

Republicans are going to be paying _very_ close attention to the final tally of our fundraising results. If they sense our campaign may be vulnerable, they’ll go to their dark money donors and tell them Virginia is in play. That will make it significantly harder for Mark to win and for Democrats to take back the Senate.

name: Katie Porter
subject: No BS. I’ll cut right to the chase.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
Peter,
I know it’s Sunday morning of a long weekend so I’ll cut right to the chase.
**[My team is racing against the clock to reach our $15,000 fundraising goal**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250831_1_ak?refcode=em20250831-40085&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=40085_3422442_HjWu4s&akid=40085%2E3422442%2EHjWu4s) before midnight tonight.
Just a few weeks ago, Kamala Harris announced she wasn’t running for California Governor and our race changed overnight.
We’re the clear Democratic fruntrunner. And it’s thanks to grassroots supporters like you, Peter. But we can’t take anything for granted, especially as Super PACs are starting to look at spending big in this race.
Can I count on your support before midnight tonight to help our campaign reach our $15,000 goal? Here’s a personal link where you can pitch in:
**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250831_1_ak?personal=dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250831_1_ak?refcode=em20250831-40085&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=40085_3422442_HjWu4s&akid=40085%2E3422442%2EHjWu4s)**

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: Our last ditch attempt at closing the gap
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
You know what’s happening, but most people still don’t. If we’re going to build a movement that stops and reverses Trump’s power grab, we have to start reaching the people who aren’t reading the news every day.
#

Peter, as I send this email we’re about 15 hours from our August fundraising deadline.
To be completely honest with you, we've still got a good way to go if we want to hit our goals. So, this is my last-ditch attempt to try and get you to make a donation.
This one isn’t really about how much we raise. The most important thing is how many donations we receive. What we’re doing every day in the Senate, in Arizona, and all across the country right now is building a movement that will stand up to everything Trump’s trying to do.
A movement like that takes time. It takes a lot of effort. But ultimately, these movements of all kinds of people from different walks of life are the only thing that work against dictators like Trump.
And yes — Trump is a dictator. We need to stop sugarcoating everything.
On my campaign, I’d often tell folks that I’d fight alone if I had to, but that we’ll always make more progress together. And the number of donations, the number of individual donors we receive are a testament to that. It’s a sign of the team we’ve built, that we are still building. There’s a lot of us — and we’re not giving up.
[**All I ask before midnight is a donation. A sign of your support as we take on more tough fights in the months ahead. Anything helps, but the donation itself is what’s important today.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-aug25.JsYhtJ&refcode=em250831-1-f&t=1&akid=3955%2E734936%2E5pO2cW)
